subject: Physics
solution:
When the first ball of mass m is projected vertically upwards, the second ball of mass 2m makes an angle θ with the vertical.

The time of flight is the same for both the balls.

Then, T1=T2 ⇒2u1g=2u2sinθg

⇒u1=u2sinθ    ...1

Maximum height of projectile is given by H=u2sin2θ2g

Ratio of the heights is H1H2=u12u22sin2θ    ...2

From equation 1 and 2, we have 

⇒H1H2=1.

Thus, x=1.

solution:

ω=2π3.14=2 rad s-1

The formula to calculate the centrifugal force on the child is given by

Fr=mω2r   ...(1)

Substitute the values of the known parameters into equation (1) to calculate the required centrifugal force.

Fr=5 kg×2 rad s-12×2 m=40 N

subject: Physics
solution:
$\theta_1=45+\alpha ; \theta_2=45-\alpha$
Time of flight, $\mathrm{T}=\frac{2 \mathrm{v} \sin \theta}{\mathrm{g}}$
$\frac{\mathrm{T}_1}{\mathrm{~T}_2}=\frac{\sin (45+\alpha)}{\sin (45-\alpha)}$
$\frac{\mathrm{T}_1}{\mathrm{~T}_2}=\frac{\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \cos \alpha+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \sin \alpha}{\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \cos \alpha-\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \sin \alpha}$
$\frac{\mathrm{T}_1}{\mathrm{~T}_2}=\frac{\cos \alpha+\sin \alpha}{\cos \alpha-\sin \alpha}=\frac{1+\tan \alpha}{1-\tan \alpha}$

subject: Physics
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Given, }\left(\mathrm{H}_{\max }\right)_1=8 \times\left(\mathrm{H}_{\max }\right)_2 \\ & \frac{\mathrm{u}^2 \sin ^2 \theta_1}{2 \mathrm{~g}}=8 \times \frac{\mathrm{u}^2 \sin ^2 \theta_2}{2 \mathrm{~g}} \\ & \Rightarrow \sin \theta_1=2 \sqrt{2} \sin \theta_2 \\ & \frac{\mathrm{~T}_1}{\mathrm{~T}_2}=\frac{2 \mathrm{u} \sin \theta_1 / \mathrm{g}}{2 \mathrm{u} \sin \theta_2 / \mathrm{g}}=\frac{\sin \theta_1}{\sin \theta_2}=2 \sqrt{2}\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
solution:
Range is same for complimentary angles. $\mathrm{T}_1=\frac{2 \mathrm{u} \sin \theta}{\mathrm{g}}$ and $\mathrm{T}_2=\frac{2 \mathrm{u} \sin (90-\theta)}{\mathrm{g}}$ and $R=\frac{u^2 \sin 2 \theta}{\mathrm{g}}$
$$
\therefore \mathrm{T}_1 \mathrm{~T}_2=\frac{2 \mathrm{u} \sin \theta}{\mathrm{g}} \times \frac{2 \mathrm{u} \cos \theta}{\mathrm{g}}=\frac{2 \mathrm{R}}{\mathrm{g}} \text {. }
$$

subject: Physics
solution:
Change in velocity,
$$
\begin{array}{l}
|\Delta \overline{\mathrm{v}}|=\sqrt{\mathrm{v}_{1}^{2}+\mathrm{v}_{2}^{2}+2 \mathrm{v}_{1} \mathrm{v}_{2} \cos (\pi-\theta)} \\
=2 \mathrm{v} \sin \frac{\theta}{2} \quad\left(\because\left|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{v}}_{1}\right|=\left|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{v}}_{2}\right|\right)=\mathrm{v} \\
=(2 \times 10) \times \sin \left(30^{\circ}\right)=2 \times 10 \times \frac{1}{2} \\
=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}
\end{array}
$$

subject: Physics
options: straight line vertically down the plane
parabola in a direction opposite to the motion of plane
parabola in the direction of motion of plane
solution: The bomb after being dropped from the plane will have only horizontal velocity but zero vertical velocity. This scenario is as same as if a body is horizontally projected from the top of a tower, and it hits the ground tracing a parabolic path. Hence, the trajectory of the bomb is also a parabola, in the direction of motion of the plane. But if we look the trajectory with respect to observer sitting in the plane then this looks like straight line vertically down the plane due to same horizontal velocity.

subject: Physics
solution:
Width of the river $d = 200$ m.
Speed of river $v_r = 18$ km/h $= 18 \times \frac{5}{18} = 5$ m/s.
Speed of boat in still water $v_b = 36$ km/h $= 36 \times \frac{5}{18} = 10$ m/s.
For minimum time to cross the river, the boat must head perpendicular to the river flow (at $90^\circ$ to the bank).
Time taken to cross once $t = \frac{d}{v_b} = \frac{200}{10} = 20$ s.
For a round trip (bank to bank and back), the total minimum time is $T = 2t = 2 \times 20 = 40$ s.
During the first crossing, the drift (displacement along the bank) is $x_1 = v_r \times t = 5 \times 20 = 100$ m.
During the return trip, to minimize time, the boat again heads perpendicular to the flow relative to the water. The river continues to flow in the same direction, so the drift is again in the same direction as the first trip.
Return drift $x_2 = v_r \times t = 5 \times 20 = 100$ m.
Total displacement along the river bank $= x_1 + x_2 = 100 + 100 = 200$ m.
Thus, minimum time is $40$ s and displacement is $200$ m.

The components of the velocity will be vy=vsin30°=40 m s-12=20 m s-1vx=vcos30°=4032 m s-1=203  m s-1

The time taken to reach maximum height is T=usin30°g=20 m s-110 m s-2=2 s

Hence, at T=2 s, as vy=0 m s-1 therefore vnet=vx=203 m s-1.

subject: Physics
solution:
Given here, u=5 m s-1, h=10 m

Using vy2=uy2+2gh in vertical direction, we have the velocity of stone in vertical direction as vy=2gh=200 m s-1   (since, uy=0).

The final velocity along the horizontal direction is the same as the initial horizontal velocity as there is no acceleration along the x-axis.

The speed with which the stone hits the ground is 

vnet=vx2+vy2=25+200=15 m s-1
